output.summary: Removed Gutenberg's default selection outline on TextCard and ImageCard by adding CSS override classes (after:!outline-none, after:!shadow-none, after:!border-none) to neutralize the ::after pseudo-element outline styles applied by WordPress editor.

filesModified.change: Added after:!outline-none after:!shadow-none after:!border-none to unconditional classes on line 54
filesModified.reason: Remove Gutenberg's default outline on ::after pseudo-element when TextCard is focused/selected in editor

filesModified.change: Added after:!outline-none after:!shadow-none after:!border-none to unconditional classes on line 82
filesModified.reason: Remove Gutenberg's default outline on ::after pseudo-element when ImageCard is focused/selected in editor

textCard.condition: Unconditional (applies to all TextCards, not just isTall)
textCard.purpose: Neutralize Gutenberg's ::after outline/shadow/border on focused blocks

imageCard.condition: Unconditional (applies to all ImageCards, not just isTall)
imageCard.purpose: Neutralize Gutenberg's ::after outline/shadow/border on focused blocks

after:!outline-none.purpose: Removes the outline property that Gutenberg applies to ::after on focused blocks
after:!outline-none.specificity: !important flag ensures override of Gutenberg's higher-specificity selector

after:!shadow-none.purpose: Removes any box-shadow that Gutenberg might apply to ::after on focused blocks

after:!border-none.purpose: Removes any border that Gutenberg might apply to ::after on focused blocks

stylesNeutralized: outline - Gutenberg applies outline styles for visual focus indication
box-shadow - May be applied for depth/focus effects
border - May be applied as part of selection outline
gutenbergDefault.note: These styles are part of Gutenberg's block selection/focus UI. We're removing them to prevent visual clutter on the card ::after pseudo-element.

unconditionalApplication.reason: Outline removal should apply to ALL cards (tall and non-tall) because the Gutenberg outline is unwanted on any card in this block
unconditionalApplication.textCard: Classes added to the always-true condition on line 54, so they apply regardless of isTall, masonryStyle, or columnCount
unconditionalApplication.imageCard: Classes added to the always-true condition on line 82, so they apply regardless of isTall, masonryStyle, or columnCount

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (asset size limits) - pre-existing, not related to changes
buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with 'webpack compiled with 3 warnings'. All warnings are pre-existing performance recommendations. TypeScript compilation passed without errors.

syncStatus.details: PHP templates intentionally left unchanged. This is editor-only styling to remove Gutenberg's selection outline. The frontend does not load Gutenberg editor styles, so no PHP changes are needed.

visualEffect.before: When a TextCard or ImageCard is focused/selected in the Gutenberg editor, Gutenberg's ::after outline appears on the block wrapper, creating unwanted visual clutter around the card
visualEffect.after: When a TextCard or ImageCard is focused/selected in the Gutenberg editor, no outline appears on the ::after pseudo-element. The card remains visually clean.
visualEffect.note: Gutenberg still provides other focus indicators (block toolbar, settings sidebar) so editors can still clearly see which block is selected

editorTesting: Open Gutenberg editor and add TextCard and ImageCard blocks
Verify no outline appears around the card when selected
Verify the block toolbar and settings sidebar still indicate which block is selected
Test with both tall and non-tall cards to ensure outline removal works for all variants
cssInspection: Inspect a selected card's computed styles in browser dev tools
frontendTesting: View the test page at https://ign.localhost/test-masonry-cards/
Verify cards display normally on the frontend (frontend doesn't have ::after selection styles anyway)
Confirm no visual regression from the CSS changes
